title: campanopsis pumilio
intro: Campanopsis pumilio, also known as the dwarf bellflower, is a flowering plant species native to mountainous regions of East Asia. Traditionally, Campanopsis pumilio has been used in herbal medicine, valued for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, treating respiratory issues, and aiding in wound healing. In herbal preparations, Campanopsis pumilio is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are typically consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
